creator: dfr@rabson.org
text: Kernel modules can add sysctl entries. If the user wishes to change the default value of those sysctls,= using sysctl.cont, they must load the kld before rc.d/sysctl runs. In practice this means loading it at boot time via /boot/loader.conf even though loading later in the boot process via rc.conf's kld_list variable is more efficient. Adding 'REQUIRE: kld' to rc.d/sysctl would make the ordering of these two scripts deterministic and allows sysctl.conf to manage sysctl variables for kernel modules loaded via kld_list.
